([/ˈdɛv pəˈtɛl/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English");[[1]](#cite_note-1) born 23 April 1990) is a British actor and filmmaker. [His accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Dev_Patel "List of awards and nominations received by Dev Patel") include a [British Academy Film Award](/wiki/British_Academy_Film_Award "British Academy Film Award"), in addition to nominations for an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award") and two [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards"). Patel was included in *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")*'s list of the [100 most influential people in the world](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") in 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actor (born 1990)

| Dev Patel | |

**Dev Patel** ([/ˈdɛv pəˈtɛl/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English");[[1]](#cite_note-1) born 23 April 1990) is a British actor and filmmaker. [His accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Dev_Patel "List of awards and nominations received by Dev Patel") include a [British Academy Film Award](/wiki/British_Academy_Film_Award "British Academy Film Award"), in addition to nominations for an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award") and two [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards"). Patel was included in *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")*'s list of the [100 most influential people in the world](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") in 2024.

Patel began his career playing [Anwar Kharral](/wiki/Anwar_Kharral "Anwar Kharral") in the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007). His [breakthrough role](/wiki/Breakthrough_role "Breakthrough role") in [Danny Boyle](/wiki/Danny_Boyle "Danny Boyle")'s drama *[Slumdog Millionaire](/wiki/Slumdog_Millionaire "Slumdog Millionaire")* (2008)[[2]](#cite_note-2) earned Patel a nomination for the [BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Leading_Role "BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role").[[3]](#cite_note-3) His career expanded with leading roles in the comedy-dramas *[The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel](/wiki/The_Best_Exotic_Marigold_Hotel "The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel")* (2011) and *[The Second Best Exotic Marigold Hotel](/wiki/The_Second_Best_Exotic_Marigold_Hotel "The Second Best Exotic Marigold Hotel")* (2015), the science fiction thriller *[Chappie](/wiki/Chappie_(film) "Chappie (film)")* (2015), and a supporting role in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") series *[The Newsroom](/wiki/The_Newsroom_(American_TV_series) "The Newsroom (American TV series)")* (2012–2014).

For his performance as [Saroo Brierley](/wiki/Saroo_Brierley "Saroo Brierley") in the drama *[Lion](/wiki/Lion_(2016_film) "Lion (2016 film)")* (2016), Patel won both the [BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Supporting_Role "BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role"), [AACTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role](/wiki/AACTA_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Supporting_Role "AACTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role") and was nominated for the [Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actor "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor"). He subsequently starred in the independent films *[Hotel Mumbai](/wiki/Hotel_Mumbai "Hotel Mumbai")* (2018), *[The Personal History of David Copperfield](/wiki/The_Personal_History_of_David_Copperfield "The Personal History of David Copperfield")* (2019) and *[The Green Knight](/wiki/The_Green_Knight_(film) "The Green Knight (film)")* (2021), and made his directorial debut with the action film *[Monkey Man](/wiki/Monkey_Man_(film) "Monkey Man (film)")* (2024).

## Early life and background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and background")]

Dev Patel was born on 23 April 1990 in [Harrow](/wiki/London_Borough_of_Harrow "London Borough of Harrow"), [North West London](/wiki/North_West_London "North West London")[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-tribute.ca-5) to Indian parents; Anita Patel, a [care worker](/wiki/Caregiver "Caregiver"), and Raju Patel, an [IT consultant](/wiki/Information_technology_consulting "Information technology consulting").[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) His parents are [Gujaratis](/wiki/Gujarati_people "Gujarati people"),[[8]](#cite_note-guardian-8) though they were both born in [Nairobi](/wiki/Nairobi "Nairobi") in [British Kenya](/wiki/British_Kenya "British Kenya"), where there is a significant [Indian diaspora](/wiki/Indians_in_Kenya "Indians in Kenya"). They immigrated to the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") separately in their teens, and first met in London.[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6)[[9]](#cite_note-latimes-9) Patel was raised in the [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism") faith.[[10]](#cite_note-yti-10) He speaks some [Gujarati](/wiki/Gujarati_language "Gujarati language").[[11]](#cite_note-11) His ancestors came from [Jamnagar](/wiki/Jamnagar "Jamnagar") and [Unjha](/wiki/Unjha "Unjha") in [Gujarat](/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

As a child, Patel became a fan of action movies after he sneaked downstairs and saw scenes from a [Bruce Lee](/wiki/Bruce_Lee "Bruce Lee") film on his parents' TV set.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Patel grew up in the [Rayners Lane](/wiki/Rayners_Lane "Rayners Lane") district of Harrow and attended Longfield Primary School and [Whitmore High School](/wiki/Whitmore_High_School "Whitmore High School"). Patel had his first acting role as [Sir Andrew Aguecheek](/wiki/Sir_Andrew_Aguecheek "Sir Andrew Aguecheek") in the school's production of *[Twelfth Night](/wiki/Twelfth_Night "Twelfth Night")*.[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6) At Whitmore High, he received an A\* in [GCSE](/wiki/General_Certificate_of_Secondary_Education "General Certificate of Secondary Education") Drama for his "self-penned portrayal of a child in the [Beslan school siege](/wiki/Beslan_school_siege "Beslan school siege")." His drama teacher Niamh Wright said, "Dev was a gifted student, who quickly impressed me with his innate ability to communicate a wide variety of characters imaginatively and creatively. He was awarded full marks for his GCSE performance to a live audience, and the visiting examiner was moved to tears by his honest portrayal."[[14]](#cite_note-14) He completed his [A Levels](/wiki/Advanced_Level_in_the_United_Kingdom "Advanced Level in the United Kingdom") in PE, Biology, History, and Drama in 2007 at Whitmore High School while working on *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*.

Patel said that he was "bloody energetic" as a child, and used to get in trouble because of it. He started training at the Rayners Lane Academy of [Taekwondo](/wiki/Taekwondo "Taekwondo") in 2000.[[15]](#cite_note-15) He competed regularly in both national and international championships, including the 2004 [AIMAA](/wiki/Action_International_Martial_Arts_Association "Action International Martial Arts Association") (Action International Martial Arts Association) World Championships in [Dublin](/wiki/Dublin "Dublin"), where he won a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal").[[9]](#cite_note-latimes-9)[[16]](#cite_note-Rayners_Lane-16)[[17]](#cite_note-irish-17) The World Championships took place in October 2004, when he competed as a [red belt](/wiki/Red_belt_(martial_arts) "Red belt (martial arts)") in the junior division against other red and [black belts](/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts) "Black belt (martial arts)"). He made it to the [semi-finals](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament"), where he lost to an Irish black belt, named Niall Fitzmaurice, in "a very close and tough fight" and ended up winning a bronze medal.[[16]](#cite_note-Rayners_Lane-16) Later, in March 2006, he earned a 1st [dan](/wiki/Dan_(rank) "Dan (rank)") black belt.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### 2006–2007: Early work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2006–2007: Early work")]

In 2006, Patel's professional acting career began when he auditioned for the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") [teen drama](/wiki/Teen_drama "Teen drama") television series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*. His mother saw the casting advertisement in *[Metro](/wiki/Metro_(British_newspaper) "Metro (British newspaper)")* and took him to the audition, even though he had a science exam the next day.[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6)[[19]](#cite_note-19) After two auditions, he was cast in the role of [Anwar Kharral](/wiki/Anwar_Kharral "Anwar Kharral"), a [British Pakistani](/wiki/British_Pakistanis "British Pakistanis") [Muslim](/wiki/Islam_in_the_United_Kingdom "Islam in the United Kingdom") teenager.[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6) The characterisation of Anwar was partly based on Patel's own personality, as the role was developed specifically for him once he was cast in *Skins*.[[20]](#cite_note-20) With no prior professional acting experience, recalled that, on "the first day of shooting, I didn't really know what to do."[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6)

The first series of the show aired in January 2007 and went on to win the [Rose d'Or](/wiki/Rose_d%27Or "Rose d'Or") for Drama in 2008 and receive a nomination for [Best Drama Series](/wiki/British_Academy_Television_Award_for_Best_Drama_Series "British Academy Television Award for Best Drama Series") at the [2008 BAFTA Television Awards](/wiki/British_Academy_Television_Awards_2008 "British Academy Television Awards 2008").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) Patel reprised his role as Anwar for the second series of *Skins*, which aired in February 2008. The second series of *Skins* won the Philips Audience Award at the [2009 BAFTA Television Awards](/wiki/British_Academy_Television_Awards_2009 "British Academy Television Awards 2009").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### 2008–2010: Breakthrough and critical acclaim

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2008–2010: Breakthrough and critical acclaim")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/aa/DevPatel08.jpg/250px-DevPatel08.jpg)](/wiki/File:DevPatel08.jpg)

Patel in 2008

Patel made his feature film debut when he was cast in the role of Jamal Malik, the central character in [Danny Boyle](/wiki/Danny_Boyle "Danny Boyle")'s film *[Slumdog Millionaire](/wiki/Slumdog_Millionaire "Slumdog Millionaire")*.[[24]](#cite_note-24) The Jamal Malik character is an Indian Muslim boy born and brought up in the [poverty](/wiki/Poverty_in_India "Poverty in India") of [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay "Bombay"), India.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Boyle considered hundreds of young male actors, but found that [Bollywood](/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") leads were generally "strong, handsome hero-types", not the personality he was looking for.[[26]](#cite_note-rags-26) Boyle's 17-year-old daughter Caitlin pointed him to *Skins*.[[26]](#cite_note-rags-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)

After five auditions for the role,[[28]](#cite_note-28) Patel was eventually cast in August 2007.[[29]](#cite_note-direct-29) The film's producer found the original choice for the lead role, [Ruslaan Mumtaz](/wiki/Ruslaan_Mumtaz "Ruslaan Mumtaz"), too good-looking for the role.[[30]](#cite_note-30) Boyle said, "I wanted a guy who didn't look like a potential hero; I wanted him to earn that in the film."[[6]](#cite_note-telegraph-6) To prepare for the role, Patel went along with Boyle while scouting for filming locations, where he was able to observe the [Dharavi](/wiki/Dharavi "Dharavi") slums for himself. He also worked at a [call-center](/wiki/Call_centre "Call centre") for a day and in a hotel, where he washed dishes.[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)

After the release of *Slumdog Millionaire* at the end of 2008, Patel went on to receive a number of awards for his performance, including a [British Independent Film Award](/wiki/British_Independent_Film_Awards_2008 "British Independent Film Awards 2008"),[[33]](#cite_note-bifa-33) [National Board of Review (NBR) Award](/wiki/National_Board_of_Review_Awards_2008 "National Board of Review Awards 2008"),[[34]](#cite_note-nbr-34) [Chicago Film Critics Association Award](/wiki/Chicago_Film_Critics_Association_Awards_2008 "Chicago Film Critics Association Awards 2008"),[[35]](#cite_note-cfcaa-35) and two [Black Reel Awards](/wiki/Black_Reel_Awards_of_2008 "Black Reel Awards of 2008") for Best Actor and Best Breakthrough Performance.[[36]](#cite_note-blackreel-36) Patel was also nominated for [Outstanding Performance by a Male Actor in a Supporting Role](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_a_Male_Actor_in_a_Supporting_Role "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by a Male Actor in a Supporting Role") at the [2009 Screen Actors Guild (SAG) Awards](/wiki/15th_Screen_Actors_Guild_Awards "15th Screen Actors Guild Awards"). The award eventually went posthumously to [Heath Ledger](/wiki/Heath_Ledger "Heath Ledger") for his performance in *[The Dark Knight](/wiki/The_Dark_Knight "The Dark Knight")*, though Patel did win the [Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_a_Cast_in_a_Motion_Picture "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture"), which he shared with ten other cast members from *Slumdog Millionaire*.[[37]](#cite_note-sagwin-37)[[38]](#cite_note-sagnom-38) On 8 January 2009, Patel won the [Critics' Choice Award](/wiki/14th_Critics%27_Choice_Awards "14th Critics' Choice Awards") for [Best Young Performer](/wiki/Broadcast_Film_Critics_Association_Award_for_Best_Young_Performer "Broadcast Film Critics Association Award for Best Young Performer").[[39]](#cite_note-Critics_Choice_Awards-39) He was also nominated for two [London Critics Circle Film Awards](/wiki/London_Film_Critics%27_Circle "London Film Critics' Circle"),[[40]](#cite_note-40) the [NAACP Image Award](/wiki/NAACP_Image_Awards "NAACP Image Awards") for [Outstanding Supporting Actor](/wiki/NAACP_Image_Award_for_Outstanding_Supporting_Actor_in_a_Motion_Picture "NAACP Image Award for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Motion Picture"),[[41]](#cite_note-naacp-41) the [2009 BAFTA Award](/wiki/62nd_British_Academy_Film_Awards "62nd British Academy Film Awards") for [Best Leading Actor](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Actor_in_a_Leading_Role "BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role"),[[42]](#cite_note-underdog-42) and [European Film Award for Best Actor](/wiki/European_Film_Award_for_Best_Actor "European Film Award for Best Actor").[[43]](#cite_note-European-43) The [film itself won](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Slumdog_Millionaire "List of awards and nominations received by Slumdog Millionaire") four [Golden Globes](/wiki/66th_Golden_Globe_Awards "66th Golden Globe Awards"),[[42]](#cite_note-underdog-42) including [Best Drama Film](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Motion_Picture_%E2%80%93_Drama "Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Drama"), and eight [Academy Awards](/wiki/81st_Academy_Awards_nominees_and_winners "81st Academy Awards nominees and winners"), including [Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture").[[44]](#cite_note-44)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bc/Dev_Patel_TIFF_2011.jpg/250px-Dev_Patel_TIFF_2011.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dev_Patel_TIFF_2011.jpg)

Patel at the [2011 Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/2011_Toronto_International_Film_Festival "2011 Toronto International Film Festival")

Patel played [Zuko](/wiki/Zuko "Zuko") in [M. Night Shyamalan](/wiki/M._Night_Shyamalan "M. Night Shyamalan")'s *[The Last Airbender](/wiki/The_Last_Airbender_(2010_film) "The Last Airbender (2010 film)")*, a feature film adaptation of the animated series *[Avatar: The Last Airbender](/wiki/Avatar:_The_Last_Airbender "Avatar: The Last Airbender")*,[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47) which was released on 1 July 2010 to extremely negative reviews. Despite being a commercial success, the film was a critical failure and Patel even received a [Razzie Award](/wiki/Razzie_Award "Razzie Award") nomination as [Worst Supporting Actor](/wiki/Golden_Raspberry_Award_for_Worst_Supporting_Actor "Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Supporting Actor") that year, although his role was well received, and was considered by many to be one of the film's positive aspects.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

Patel later starred in the short film *The Commuter*,[[49]](#cite_note-49) which was directed by [McHenry Brothers](/wiki/The_McHenry_Brothers "The McHenry Brothers") to promote the [Nokia N8](/wiki/Nokia_N8 "Nokia N8") smartphone in the UK[[50]](#cite_note-50) Fans who won a Nokia UK run competition starred alongside Dev Patel in the short film.[[51]](#cite_note-51)[[52]](#cite_note-52)

### 2011–2020: Established actor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2011–2020: Established actor")]

Patel co-starred in *[The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel](/wiki/The_Best_Exotic_Marigold_Hotel "The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel")* (2012), (and subsequently in its 2015 sequel, *[The Second Best Exotic Marigold Hotel](/wiki/The_Second_Best_Exotic_Marigold_Hotel "The Second Best Exotic Marigold Hotel")*) directed by [John Madden](/wiki/John_Madden_(director) "John Madden (director)"), which received positive reviews[[53]](#cite_note-53) and was a box office success, grossing $136 million.[[54]](#cite_note-54) For the role, he had to take lessons in perfecting an Indian-English accent.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

From 2012 to 2014, Patel had a supporting role in the 2012 [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") television series *[The Newsroom](/wiki/The_Newsroom_(American_TV_series) "The Newsroom (American TV series)")* as Neal Sampat, blogger for news anchor Will McAvoy.[[56]](#cite_note-56) He also appeared alongside [James Franco](/wiki/James_Franco "James Franco") and [Heather Graham](/wiki/Heather_Graham "Heather Graham") in *[About Cherry](/wiki/About_Cherry "About Cherry")*, which premiered at the [2012 Berlin International Film Festival](/wiki/2012_Berlin_International_Film_Festival "2012 Berlin International Film Festival"). In 2014, Patel starred alongside [Robert Sheehan](/wiki/Robert_Sheehan "Robert Sheehan") and [Zoë Kravitz](/wiki/Zo%C3%AB_Kravitz "Zoë Kravitz") in the film, *[The Road Within](/wiki/The_Road_Within "The Road Within")*, about three unlikely friends with various disabilities who go on a road trip. The film received generally mixed reviews. *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* had positive words for the "bristling and committed performances by Robert Sheehan, Dev Patel and Zoe Kravitz" while noting that "there remains a nagging tidiness to the whole endeavor that leaves a strained, cloying aftertaste" that kept the movie from truly succeeding.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

In 2015, Patel acted in *[Chappie](/wiki/Chappie_(film) "Chappie (film)")* as an engineer who helps design police robots and as the mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan in the biopic *[The Man Who Knew Infinity](/wiki/The_Man_Who_Knew_Infinity_(film) "The Man Who Knew Infinity (film)")*. In 2016, Patel starred as [Saroo Brierley](/wiki/Saroo_Brierley "Saroo Brierley") in the biographical film *[Lion](/wiki/Lion_(2016_film) "Lion (2016 film)")*, directed by [Garth Davis](/wiki/Garth_Davis "Garth Davis") and co-starring [Nicole Kidman](/wiki/Nicole_Kidman "Nicole Kidman") and [Rooney Mara](/wiki/Rooney_Mara "Rooney Mara"), which premiered to rave reviews and "[Oscar](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") buzz" at the [2016 Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/2016_Toronto_International_Film_Festival "2016 Toronto International Film Festival").[[58]](#cite_note-58)[[59]](#cite_note-:0-59)[[60]](#cite_note-60)[[61]](#cite_note-61) The film is based on Brierley's memoir *[A Long Way Home](/wiki/A_Long_Way_Home_(book) "A Long Way Home (book)")*.[[62]](#cite_note-62) Patel was nominated for Best Supporting Actor for his role at the [70th British Academy Film Awards](/wiki/70th_British_Academy_Film_Awards "70th British Academy Film Awards") and the [89th Academy Awards](/wiki/89th_Academy_Awards "89th Academy Awards"), winning at the former. He is the third actor of Indian descent to receive an Oscar nomination.[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)

In 2018, Patel starred in the action-thriller *[Hotel Mumbai](/wiki/Hotel_Mumbai "Hotel Mumbai")* and *[The Wedding Guest](/wiki/The_Wedding_Guest_(2018_film) "The Wedding Guest (2018 film)")*. In 2019, Patel starred as the titular character in [Armando Iannucci](/wiki/Armando_Iannucci "Armando Iannucci")'s adaptation of [Charles Dickens](/wiki/Charles_Dickens "Charles Dickens")' *[The Personal History of David Copperfield](/wiki/The_Personal_History_of_David_Copperfield "The Personal History of David Copperfield")*, for which he received a nomination for the 2021 Golden Globe Award for Best Actor – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66) He also returned to television in an episode of the [Amazon Prime](/wiki/Amazon_Prime "Amazon Prime") anthology *[Modern Love](/wiki/Modern_Love_(TV_series) "Modern Love (TV series)")*, for which he received an [Emmy](/wiki/Emmy "Emmy") nomination.

### 2020–present: Expansion into directing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2020–present: Expansion into directing")]

In 2021, Patel starred in *[The Green Knight](/wiki/The_Green_Knight_(film) "The Green Knight (film)")*, based on *[Sir Gawain and the Green Knight](/wiki/Sir_Gawain_and_the_Green_Knight "Sir Gawain and the Green Knight"),* directed by [David Lowery](/wiki/David_Lowery_(director) "David Lowery (director)").[[67]](#cite_note-67)[[68]](#cite_note-moshakis2021-68) In April of that year, he signed a deal to "produce, develop and create projects" with ShivHans Pictures.[[69]](#cite_note-69) Towards the end of 2021, Patel established his own production company, Minor Realm.[[70]](#cite_note-70) In 2023, Patel appeared in [Wes Anderson](/wiki/Wes_Anderson "Wes Anderson")'s *[The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar](/wiki/The_Wonderful_Story_of_Henry_Sugar_(film) "The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar (film)")*, an adaptation of a [short story](/wiki/The_Wonderful_Story_of_Henry_Sugar_and_Six_More "The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar and Six More") by [Roald Dahl](/wiki/Roald_Dahl "Roald Dahl"), starring opposite [Benedict Cumberbatch](/wiki/Benedict_Cumberbatch "Benedict Cumberbatch"), [Ralph Fiennes](/wiki/Ralph_Fiennes "Ralph Fiennes"), and [Ben Kingsley](/wiki/Ben_Kingsley "Ben Kingsley").[[71]](#cite_note-71)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9b/SXSW_2024_-_Dev_Patel_2_%28cropped-J1%29.jpg/250px-SXSW_2024_-_Dev_Patel_2_%28cropped-J1%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:SXSW_2024_-_Dev_Patel_2_(cropped-J1).jpg)

Patel at the [South by Southwest Festival](/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest") (SXSW) 2024

Patel made his directorial debut with the 2024 action thriller film *[Monkey Man](/wiki/Monkey_Man_(film) "Monkey Man (film)")*, as announced in 2018. In addition to directing, Patel stars in the film, and is credited as a co-writer and producer.[[72]](#cite_note-Deadline-72)[[73]](#cite_note-73) The film was initially set to be distributed by [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix"),[[74]](#cite_note-74) but [Jordan Peele](/wiki/Jordan_Peele "Jordan Peele"), having seen the film, felt that it deserved a theatrical release instead, so he acquired it from Netflix under his [Monkeypaw Productions](/wiki/Monkeypaw_Productions "Monkeypaw Productions") banner.[[75]](#cite_note-Monkey_Man-75) *Monkey Man* had its world premiere at [South by Southwest](/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest") on 11 March 2024.[[76]](#cite_note-Universal-76) It had its [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") premiere on 2 April 2024,[[77]](#cite_note-77) and was released in Australian cinemas on 4 April 2024.[[78]](#cite_note-78) It was released by [Universal Pictures](/wiki/Universal_Pictures "Universal Pictures") in the US on 5 April 2024.[[76]](#cite_note-Universal-76)

Patel will executive produce and star in the [Miramax](/wiki/Miramax "Miramax") miniseries *The Key Man* as disgraced businessman [Arif Naqvi](/wiki/Arif_Naqvi "Arif Naqvi"),[[79]](#cite_note-79) and was initially set to join [Olivia Colman](/wiki/Olivia_Colman "Olivia Colman") in *[Wicker](/wiki/Wicker_(film) "Wicker (film)")* before dropping out.[[80]](#cite_note-80)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dev_Patel&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Patel began dating his *[Slumdog Millionaire](/wiki/Slumdog_Millionaire "Slumdog Millionaire")* co-star [Freida Pinto](/wiki/Freida_Pinto "Freida Pinto") in 2009.[[81]](#cite_note-81) On 10 December 2014, the couple announced that they had split after nearly six years of dating.[[82]](#cite_note-82) In March 2017, Patel's relationship with [Australian](/wiki/Australia "Australia") actress [Tilda Cobham-Hervey](/wiki/Tilda_Cobham-Hervey "Tilda Cobham-Hervey") became public. They met nine months earlier on the set of *[Hotel Mumbai](/wiki/Hotel_Mumbai "Hotel Mumbai")*.[[83]](#cite_note-83) In April 2022 they moved to Cobham-Hervey's home town, [Adelaide](/wiki/Adelaide "Adelaide").[[84]](#cite_note-84) The couple posed for photographs together for the first time on the [red carpet](/wiki/Red_carpet "Red carpet") at the [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") premiere of Patel's debut feature as a director, *[Monkey Man](/wiki/Monkey_Man_(film) "Monkey Man (film)")*, on 2 April 2024.[[85]](#cite_note-85)

In August 2022, Patel featured in the local news after he broke up a fight in [Gouger Street](/wiki/Gouger_Street "Gouger Street") in [Adelaide city centre](/wiki/Adelaide_city_centre "Adelaide city centre").[[86]](#cite_note-86)
